editableNewDialogue.text: Did you see that Four Eyes tie-in starting up at the centre? The promo stand looks like it’s teasing something big.
Yeah—between the CITV branding and the little “coming soon” screens, it feels like a launch event rather than just a one-off appearance. Are people saying it’s for new mascots?
Aren’t those the blobs from the Xmas ad? I swear I saw the same gooey shapes on the posters.
That’s what it sounds like. If they’re reusing the characters, it’s probably deliberate: first cameo in the Christmas spot, then a proper unveiling once the partnership kicks off.
Thing is, I can’t work out what happened with the old mascot. It’s like he just… stopped showing up.
If there’s no official send-off, people tend to read into gaps. Sometimes brands do a soft swap: reduce appearances, then quietly replace. It can feel odd when there’s no announcement.
Silver only appeared during the Christmas Party, so I read nothing into Xmas 2008, even after Gloopy and Glitter appeared in the ads. When Silver didn't appear in the party I felt weird.

That makes sense—if the Christmas Party was the one “guaranteed” spot to see him, then missing it feels like a signal. If the Four Eyes event fully introduces the blobs, that’ll basically confirm the handover, even if they never say it outright.

User12 (MallMum84):
Seeing all this mascot talk has me flashing back to that Tesco café mess—went to grab the kids’ hot chocolates and the till kept freezing, so the queue just… didn’t move.

User13 (QueueWatcher):
Oh I remember that day. Folk were bailing to the food court because the card reader was having none of it—felt like the whole place was stuck buffering.

Thread Title: Quick correction on what this thread’s actually about

—

User1 (GlasgowGlitter):
Sorry to be that person, but this isn’t really “discussion on the evolution of Silverburn’s mascots and advertising campaigns” as a general thing.

It’s specifically folk chatting about the 2007 TV-tree Christmas ad, then the 2008 Gloopy & Glitter love-story ad (and the weird Toonattik debut), then the in-centre screen throwbacks and the kids club rumours/launches.

So it’s more “timeline of Silverburn’s Xmas ads + mascots” than a broad discussion.

—

User2 (YuleTideYoda):
Aye, agreed. The whole thread’s basically us tracking which Christmas spots aired on STV and which ones kept looping on the advert screens in-centre.

Like, it’s mostly: TV-tree (2007), blobs love story (2008), then later years getting skipped in-centre, and then the mascots stuff branching off from that.

—

User3 (MistletoeMaggie):
Exactly. It’s not a big marketing deep-dive, it’s us noticing what got replayed, what vanished, and how that lined up with Silver disappearing and the blobs getting pushed.

—

annotation.queryText: I’m brainstorming a sequel to that fictional brand’s ad-universe we talked about. If I want the holiday TV schedule to feel “stuck in time” (like only one specific era of seasonal spots keep looping and even start functioning as channel idents), what earlier thread from our discussion would explain why that would be the most fitting creative choice—and which recurring character would I naturally feature much more heavily in that follow-up?

subAnnotations.queryText: Just saw the new Silverburn summer campaign ads on the centre screens - they've got this retro pixel art theme with little aliens collecting shopping bags. Cute, but it's got me thinking... remember when we first saw those blobby shapes in the 2008 Christmas ads? Back then nobody knew they'd become Gloopy and Glitter. Now they're using similar amorphous designs again. Do you think this is them testing new mascot concepts, or just nostalgic callback? Feels like we're watching history repeat itself in real time.
